Looking forward to earning your trust on this one.

Best,
Mike Ross

ground_truth.correct_segment: The email fails on multiple dimensions simultaneously, making it a hard multi-thread-conflict case. (1) HONESTY VIOLATION – PRIMARY FAILURE: The email falsely claims ProjectFlow supports 'Single Sign-On SAML' – this is explicitly on the do-not-claim list because OAuth is current state. This is a material misrepresentation to a technical evaluator who will discover the falsehood during due diligence. (2) GROUNDING VIOLATION: The email introduces 'per-seat pricing' which directly contradicts the bench summary stating the model is per-project, not per-seat. This will create a trust-breaking discrepancy when Tom or Kate reviews a proposal. (3) MULTI-THREAD-CONFLICT VIOLATION: The prior thread clearly shows Sam already committed to two specific deliverables (SSO spec for Tom, StartupHub reference call intro) that are now 4 days overdue. The email completely ignores these missed commitments, does not acknowledge the delay, does not deliver the spec, and pivots to DevTeam instead of the promised StartupHub reference. This breaks continuity with the prior thread and will read as dismissive to a skeptical Tom. (4) ICP VIOLATION: The email is addressed only to Lisa, bypassing Tom who is the active technical stakeholder with outstanding deliverables and the decision to include only Lisa ignores the multi-threaded buying committee dynamic established in the discovery call. (5) TONE VIOLATION: The opening 'exciting updates' and 'couldn't be better' is inappropriately buoyant given that trust has already been damaged by missed deliverables; the relationship requires an accountability-first tone before re-pitching.
